title: shut down my laptop since it was running super slow, my entire firefox history and bookmarks were wiped
content:
for context: my laptop was working/running completely fine last night, as of about midnight (east coast). booted it up around noon today to see that it was super slow and kinda laggy (this is a windows problem, not a firefox one, don't try and fix that, i know it sucks), and that if i restarted/shut down, it would automatically update (not what i wanted). when i did finally begrudgingly restart my laptop, it auto-updated as windows does, and i expected to open up firefox and go on with my day.

unfortunately, it prompted me to make a new user. and when i signed into my firefox account, it treated the browser that i have used for at least 8 months like i had freshly downloaded it. all of my history on here is gone. all of my bookmarks are gone. when i connected my phone to try and sync from what i had left before i updated, all my sync information went down the drain and is completely gone as well. i really don't understand what went wrong here, and i'm honestly devastated that all of my work and my history has to be painstakingly rebuilt.

if anyone has any idea of what happened here, it'd make me feel a little better and try to avoid it in the future.
creator: heyhelloitsmilo

title: Tab groups randomly gone a day or so ago
content:
So, today I found that absolutely all of my tab groups have been wiped from existence. Not passwords, bookmarks, history, extensions or anything of the sort. Just tab groups specifically. Everything else in my profile is still here. I tried restoring from a backup using the Session History Scrounger at https://www.jeffersonscher.com/ffu/scrounger.html but my closest backup was a March 26th one that is missing an entire tab group created after that date.

I tried searching other solutions to my issue, but nothing has worked so far. It doesn't have anything to do with updates as the last one happened on March 26th and I definitely used the tab groups after that point. I'm at a complete loss on what I am supposed to do, as I lost ~600 tabs combined across all groups

title: I’m having trouble interacting with the Ohm Energie website.
content: Hello, I'm French and I'm not very good at English I’ve been a Mozilla user for many years and am very satisfied with it. I’m having trouble interacting with the Ohm Energie website. I wanted to sign up for an electricity plan. The Ohm Energie page opened fine in my browser, but as soon as I tried to interact with it—for example, to request a quote—I received the following security notification: “Firefox has detected a potentially serious security issue with espace-client.ohm-energie.com. Someone impersonating the site might try to steal things like credit card information, passwords, or email addresses.” I was forced to use Chrome to sign up for my plan. I’m not the only Firefox user facing this issue. I called Ohm Energie and was told that many other customers have also encountered this problem. I’d like to be able to access my Ohm Energie account via Firefox—what can I do? I should mention that I’m not very experienced in resolving this type of issue. Thank you for your help

title: EDGE-A-001
content: El Poder de la Evidencia Visual En el mundo profesional, una imagen no solo vale más que mil palabras, sino que ahorra horas de correos de ida y vuelta. Tu captura original es un gran comienzo, pero para hacerla "nivel experto", podrías aplicar estas técnicas: El Recuadro de Atención: Si el problema es que el cuadro de texto está bloqueado, dibujar un círculo rojo sobre él en la imagen guía el ojo del técnico instantáneamente. Contexto del Sistema: Tu captura muestra la barra de estado (hora, red, batería). ¡Eso es oro! Le dice al técnico que estás en un móvil, con buena señal y qué hora era. Anotaciones Breves: Escribir un pequeño texto sobre la imagen (ej: "El cursor no aparece aquí") elimina cualquier duda. Escenario de Práctica: "El Pantallazo Maestro" 📸 Imagina que estás en una reunión de Zoom o Teams y la opción de "Compartir Pantalla" te aparece en gris (deshabilitada). Quieres enviarle una captura a tu jefe para demostrar que no es que no quieras presentar, sino que el sistema no te deja. ¿Qué elementos incluirías en esa captura de pantalla para que sea una evidencia irrefutable? (Piensa en qué botones, menús o mensajes de error deberían salir en la foto).

title: Can one stop redirection blocking and prevent reloading the page when viewing source as well?
content:
The Firefox default behaviour to reload a page for view-source even when it is already in memory is unnecessary and causes many difficulties when tracking CGI bugs.

This unnecessary reloading can be turned off by setting accessibility.blockautorefresh to true.

However, fixing these stupid page reloads this way introduces another hyper-annoying behaviour: then Firefox blocks redirects.

So this accessibility setting is bad in both possible settings. Is there a possibility to prevent reloading pages for view-source while still allowing redirects?
